content:
由 Boxue 启动一次 AutoGen 多Agent科研协作闭环验证。创建科学研究项目，标题为『4.5V以上高压锂电池机制与假设验证』，领域为『High-voltage lithium batteries above 4.5V, cathode/electrolyte/interface stability, AI-assisted battery materials discovery』，目标是验证从文献阅读、知识图谱构建、Gap探索、假设生成、苏格拉底辩论、机制审计到最终裁决的多Agent协作能力。请按以下闭环执行：1. Boxue 作为中央调度者，创建项目并启动 research_hypothesis_debate / AutoGen GroupChat 协作流，参与角色必须包括：   - Boxue：中央调度与最终仲裁   - ZhiZhi：文献检索、导入、结构化抽取、PaperGraph构建   - TanXi：知识缺口识别与优先级排序   - MingLi：基于Gap生成可证伪科研假设   - DuZhi：苏格拉底式质询与反例挑战   - YanZhen：机制一致性、证据一致性、域迁移/CAWM审计   - BianLun：多轮辩论裁判与最终裁决2. ZhiZhi 先进行分层级联文献检索，providers 只使用 ['semantic_scholar','arxiv','chemrxiv']，max_results=20，import_top_k=10。检索主题围绕：   - high-voltage lithium batteries above 4.5V   - LiCoO2 high voltage cathode   - Ni-rich layered oxide high voltage stability   - electrolyte oxidation at high voltage   - cathode electrolyte interphase CEI   - surface coating and doping for high-voltage cathodes   - thermal stability and oxygen release   - AI-assisted battery materials discovery3. 文献导入必须尽量覆盖以下层级：   - L0：高影响综述，优先导入至少1篇   - L1：里程碑/高被引基础论文，优先导入至少1篇   - L2：近2-3年顶刊或高质量最新成果，优先导入至少1篇   - L3：arXiv/ChemRxiv等预印本前沿，优先导入至少1篇   - L4：常规补充论文   如果某层级候选不足，请记录原因并将配额转移到下一层；不要把低相关论文强行导入。4. ZhiZhi 需要执行结构化抽取：   - method   - scenario   - benchmark   - contribution   - limitation / future work   - keynote / mechanism summary   - quality_flags   如果摘要缺失、只有 Conclusion:、或大量 unknown，请触发修复或标记 extraction_quality，并尽量使用 conclusion/full_text_excerpt 补充。5. 如果 domain_coverage_diagnostic 发现盲区，例如 electrolyte chemistry、CEI formation、oxygen release、surface reconstruction、thermal runaway、high-voltage cycling degradation、coating/doping trade-off，并且 live_probe 显示存在文献，请自动执行小配额补全文献，不要等待人工确认。补全文献后更新 PaperGraph。6. TanXi 基于 PaperGraph 进行 gap 探索。请优先识别：   - 机制型Gap：例如高电压下结构相变、氧释放、电解液氧化、CEI失效之间的因果链不清   - 矛盾型Gap：例如某些涂层/掺杂策略在一篇文献中提升稳定性，但另一篇文献指出阻碍Li+传输或导致副反应   - 边界条件Gap：例如4.5V、4.6V、4.7V以上性能是否由温度、电解液、循环倍率、粒径或截止电压决定   - 迁移型Gap：例如AI模型/高通量筛选是否能从低电压体系迁移到高电压失效机制预测   不要只依赖 method-scenario-benchmark 的机械缺失组合。7. MingLi 从排名最高且语义合理的Gap生成1-2个科研假设。每个假设必须包含：   - 清晰标题   - 核心假设   - 具体机制链：输入/干预 -> 中介机制 -> 可观测输出   - 可控变量，例如涂层厚度、掺杂浓度、电解液添加剂浓度、截止电压、温度、循环倍率   - 可测指标，例如容量保持率、阻抗增长、CEI组成、氧释放量、相变程度、倍率性能、热稳定性   - 基线与对照组   - 可证伪条件   - 与 PaperGraph 证据的对应关系   禁止使用空泛模板，例如『X改变信息/干预/表示通路』。8. DuZhi 和 BianLun 启动至少5轮三角闭环辩论：   - R1：DuZhi提出概念澄清和因果链质询，MingLi逐条回应并修订   - R2：YanZhen执行内部一致性和数据一致性审计，DuZhi基于审计结果继续追问，MingLi修订   - R3：DuZhi提出反例与域迁移测试，例如更高截止电压、升高温度、高倍率、不同电解液体系、工业级载量条件   - R4：如果 YanZhen 标记 unsupported_claims 或 required_actions，则触发 ZhiZhi 定向补充检索，再由 MingLi重写或收缩假设   - R5：BianLun 输出最终裁决：accept_for_experiment / revise / human_review / reject9. YanZhen 必须输出机制审计报告，包含：   - verdict: PASS / REQUIRES_REVISION / REJECTED   - unsupported_claims   - required_actions   - feasibility_audit   - layer_1_internal_consistency   - layer_2_data_consistency   - layer_3_regime_shift_test   - overall_verdict   如果发现假设缺少可观测量、可控变量、边界条件，必须要求修订。10. 最终输出请汇总：   - project_id   - AutoGen run_id / groupchat_id   - 导入文献数量与各L层数量   - 是否发生盲区自动补全   - TanXi识别的Top gaps   - MingLi最终假设标题和核心机制链   - DuZhi提出的最高严重性问题   - YanZhen verdict、unsupported_claims、required_actions   - BianLun final_decision   - debate_state 是否包含 arguments、revisions、mechanism_audits、literature_supplements   - 如果闭环未通过，请明确卡在哪一步，以及下一步应补什么。

    "abstract": "The correlation between the electrochemical performance and the electronic structure and chemical composition of the cathode/electrolyte-and anode/electrolyte-interfaces in all-solid-state batteries (ASSB) is systematically studied. ASSBs are composed of LiCoPO4 (LCP) or LiCoO2 (redox potential ~4.8 V and ~3.8 V vs Li+/Li, respectively) thin-film cathode materials, Li1+xAlxTi2-x(PO4)3 (LATP) as the solid-state electrolyte and lithium metal as the anode. X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) interface experiments on LCP thin-film deposition on the LATP surface reveal a partial reduction of Ti4+ ions attributed to electronic charge transfer from LCP to LATP without the involvement of the PO4 polyanion in the process. Electrochemical activity of the ASSBs is primarily limited by the anode/electrolyte interface rather than the cathode/electrolyte interface. Post-cycling XPS analysis of the Li∣LATP interface indicates lateral and in-depth chemical inhomogeneity with a strong change in PO4 polyanionic chemical environment and Li+ accumulation at the areas with a better electrolyte/anode contact. Engineering the Li∣LATP interface via coating of the LATP surface with lithium oxynitride (LiPON) or a LiTFSI-PEO polymer electrolyte, or by wetting the LATP with the liquid electrolytes, significantly improves battery cycling stability even without artificial interface modification at the cathode side. The optimized Li∣LiTFSI-PEO∣LATP∣LCP cells showed excellent cycling performance between 3.0 V and 5.0 V at a charging time of 12 min (≈ 5C rate). Stable cycling was sustained over 10 cycles, followed by 30 and 50 additional cycles at reduced charging rates (≈ 4C and 1C, respectively). Good electrochemical cyclability was further demonstrated with an upper cut-off voltage of 5.3 V vs. Li+/Li.",

    "abstract": "The development of next-generation electrochemical energy storage requires devices that combine the high energy density of batteries with the power capability and long cycle life of supercapacitors. However, the interfacial phenomena governing performance in these systems remain poorly unified. The solid-electrolyte interphase (SEI), a nanoscale film formed by electrolyte decomposition, is well studied in batteries but its counterpart in supercapacitors has received limited systematic investigation despite growing experimental evidence. This review argues that SEI formation is a universal electrochemical process that occurs whenever electrode potentials drive electron transfer into electrolyte orbitals beyond their stability limits, independent of whether charge storage is Faradaic or non-Faradaic. Differences between battery SEIs and supercapacitor interphases arise mainly from operating conditions, not fundamental chemistry. Engineered interphases created through electrolyte additives, protective coatings, or surface functionalization suppress leakage currents, improve capacitance retention, and enable stable high-voltage operation. By identifying shared mechanisms and establishing transferable design rules, this unified framework provides a foundation for predictive interphase engineering that supports long-lived, high-performance energy-storage technologies.",

    "abstract": "The development of lithium–metal batteries (LMBs) has emerged as a mainstream approach for achieving high‐energy‐density energy storage devices. The stability of electrochemical interfaces plays an essential role in realizing stable and long‐life LMBs. Despite extensive and comprehensive research on the lithium anode interface, there is limited focus on the cathode interface, particularly regarding high‐voltage transition metal oxide cathode materials. In this review, the challenges associated with developing stable high‐voltage cathode materials are first discussed. Characterization techniques for understanding the composition and structure of the cathode–electrolyte interphase (CEI) are then introduced. Subsequently, recent developments in electrolyte design and interface modification for constructing a stable CEI are summarized. Finally, perspectives on future research trends for understanding and developing the high‐voltage CEI are discussed. This review can offer valuable guidance for understanding and designing the high‐voltage CEI, pushing forward the development of high‐energy‐density LMBs.",

    "abstract": "Fluorinated ethers have become promising electrolyte solvent candidates for lithium metal batteries (LMBs) because they are endowed with high oxidative stability and high Coulombic efficiencies of lithium metal stripping/plating. Up to now, most reported fluorinated ether electrolytes are -CF3-based, and the influence of ion solvation in modifying degree of fluorination has not been well-elucidated. In this work, we synthesize a hexacyclic coordinated ether (1-methoxy-3-ethoxypropane, EMP) and its fluorinated ether counterparts with -CH2F (F1EMP), -CHF2 (F2EMP), or -CF3 (F3EMP) as terminal group. With lithium bis(fluorosulfonyl)imide as single salt, the solvation structure, Li-ion transport behavior, lithium deposition kinetics, and high-voltage stability of the electrolytes were systematically studied. Theoretical calculations and spectra reveal the gradually reduced solvating power from nonfluorinated EMP to fully fluorinated F3EMP, which leads to decreased ionic conductivity. In contrast, the weakly solvating fluorinated ethers possess higher Li+ transference number and exchange current density. Overall, partially fluorinated -CHF2 is demonstrated as the desired group. Further full cell testing using high-voltage (4.4 V) and high-loading (3.885 mAh cm-2) LiNi0.8Co0.1Mn0.1O2 cathode demonstrates that F2EMP electrolyte enables 80% capacity retention after 168 cycles under limited Li (50 μm) and lean electrolyte (5 mL Ah-1) conditions and 129 cycles under extremely lean electrolyte (1.8 mL Ah-1) and the anode-free conditions. This work deepens the fundamental understanding on the ion transport and interphase dynamics under various degrees of fluorination and provides a feasible approach toward the design of fluorinated ether electrolytes for practical high-voltage LMBs.",
